info.title: peryx
info.description: Read-through cache and private index for multiple ecosystems. A PyPI index serves the Simple API under `/{route}/`, where `{route}` is the index's route (for example `root/pypi`); an OCI index serves the distribution-spec registry under `/v2/`. Write operations authenticate with HTTP Basic where the password is the target hosted index's upload token and the username is ignored.

servers.description: A local peryx with the default configuration

get.description: A compact server document with global URLs and one discovery entry per configured index. It is built from configuration and request context, without reading package indexes.

Searches the derived package index built from cached simple pages, local uploads, and cached core metadata. `q` uses substring matching; prefix it with `re:` for a regex. Index policy removes denied packages before indexing. Results are sorted by display name and paged without collecting every match.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search text. Prefix with `re:` to use a regex.

results.summary: A simple framework for building complex web applications.

get.description: Counters aggregated off the request path, drillable: no parameters for per-index totals, `?index={route}` for one index's projects, `&project={name}` for one project's files. Counters are grouped by the role that owns them: a neutral `base` group every index reports, a `cached` group only a caching index fills, a `hosted` group only an upload store fills, and an `ecosystem` map of the driver's own counters (PyPI's PEP 658 sibling under `metadata`).

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: With `index`, drill into one project's files

200.description: The counters at the requested depth

get.description: Version, counters, and the configured indexes. Add `?details=admin` for bounded metadata summaries used by the read-only admin status page.

parameters.name: details
parameters.description: Use `admin` to include observed project counts, uploaded file counts, and recent uploads.

200.description: The OpenAPI 3.1 description of this server

text/plain; version=0.0.4.example:
# HELP peryx_requests_total Total HTTP requests served.
# TYPE peryx_requests_total counter
peryx_requests_total 128
# HELP peryx_index_metadata_total PEP 658 metadata siblings served.
# TYPE peryx_index_metadata_total counter
peryx_index_metadata_total{index="root/pypi",ecosystem="pypi",role="virtual"} 37

get.description: Confirms this is an OCI distribution-spec registry. Answers `200` with `Docker-Distribution-API-Version: registry/2.0` and an empty body.

post.summary: Begin, mount, or monolithically push a blob

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The repository name, carrying the OCI index route as a prefix

202.description: A session opened for chunked upload

parameters.description: The repository name, carrying the OCI index route as a prefix

parameters.description: The repository name, carrying the OCI index route as a prefix

416.description: The chunk is out of order

get.description: Serves the blob from the content-addressed store, pulling it through an online proxy member on a miss; concurrent misses share one upstream fetch. Range-capable.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The repository name, carrying the OCI index route as a prefix

parameters.name: digest
parameters.description: A content digest; blob digests must be `sha256:...`

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The repository name, carrying the OCI index route as a prefix

parameters.name: digest
parameters.description: A content digest; blob digests must be `sha256:...`

get.description: peryx's own layer browser (not a distribution-spec route): lists a stored layer's tar members, or with `?member=` previews one text member in bounded chunks.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The repository name, carrying the OCI index route as a prefix

parameters.name: digest
parameters.description: A content digest; blob digests must be `sha256:...`

200.description: The member list, or one member's text

404.description: The blob or member is unknown

get.description: Resolves the reference hosted-first through the index's members and serves the manifest, pulling it through an online proxy member on a miss.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The repository name, carrying the OCI index route as a prefix

put.description: Stores the manifest under its canonical `sha256:` digest and, for a tag reference, points the tag at it. Requires a writable hosted index and its upload token.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The repository name, carrying the OCI index route as a prefix

403.description: `DENIED`: read-only index or blocked by policy

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The repository name, carrying the OCI index route as a prefix

get.description: The manifests that declare `{digest}` as their subject (attestations, signatures).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The repository name, carrying the OCI index route as a prefix

parameters.name: digest
parameters.description: A content digest; blob digests must be `sha256:...`

get.description: Answers `{"name", "tags"}`, with `n`/`last` pagination and a `Link` next-page header.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The repository name, carrying the OCI index route as a prefix

post.description: The legacy PyPI upload API, as sent by `twine upload` and `uv publish`. The multipart form's `content` part carries a wheel or a `.tar.gz` or `.zip` sdist. peryx streams the bytes to a staged blob, verifies declared `sha256_digest` and `blake2_256_digest` values, then checks filename, archive, wheel `.dist-info` structure, RECORD hashes, and sdist top-level structure before the file lands in the index's local layer. Uploaded wheels and sdists get PEP 658/714 `.metadata` siblings from verified core metadata. The upload shadows any upstream file of the same name.

text/plain.example: metadata Name "other" does not match upload name "peryxpkg"

403.description: Uploads disabled, project status rejects uploads, or index policy denied the upload

405.description: The route's index has no writable local layer

get.description: A compact index document with URLs and client configuration snippets. Snippets appear only when the request has enough host context to render absolute URLs.

client_configuration..pypirc:
[distutils]
index-servers =
    peryx

[peryx]
repository = http://127.0.0.1:4433/root/pypi/
username = __token__
password = <upload-token>
client_configuration.uv.toml:
publish-url = "http://127.0.0.1:4433/root/pypi/"

[[index]]
name = "peryx"
url = "http://127.0.0.1:4433/root/pypi/simple/"
default = true

[pip]
index-url = "http://127.0.0.1:4433/root/pypi/simple/"

get.description: Searches the derived package index built from cached simple pages, local uploads, and cached core metadata. `q` uses substring matching; prefix it with `re:` for a regex. Index policy removes denied packages before indexing. Results are sorted by display name and paged without collecting every match.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search text. Prefix with `re:` to use a regex.

results.summary: A simple framework for building complex web applications.

get.description: Serves the blob if cached; otherwise fetches it from its upstream cache, verifies the sha256, caches it, and serves it. The `{filename}` segment is display context and must be percent-encoded as one path segment. Responses are immutable (`Cache-Control: max-age=31536000`).

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: The display filename, percent-encoded as one path segment. Separators, traversal, and control characters are rejected.

400.description: The digest is not 64 lowercase hex, or the filename is not a safe path segment

403.description: Project status or index policy does not allow downloads

404.description: No file with this digest is known

502.description: The upstream cache failed or the bytes did not match the digest

get.description: The `.metadata` sibling of an artifact: advertised upstream metadata verified against the index-page digest, or metadata generated from a wheel `METADATA`/sdist `PKG-INFO` when upstream omits the sibling. pip and uv resolve through this instead of downloading whole artifacts.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: The display filename, percent-encoded as one path segment. Separators, traversal, and control characters are rejected.

403.description: Project status or index policy does not allow downloads

404.description: The artifact has no known metadata sibling

get.description: The file members of a cached wheel, zip, zipped egg, plain tarball, or gzip tarball (`.tar.gz` and `.tgz`). Nested archives are not expanded unless the request names them with repeated `container` query parameters. Pass `member` to read one bounded text chunk.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: The display filename, percent-encoded as one path segment. Separators, traversal, and control characters are rejected.

parameters.name: container
parameters.description: Optional archive-member path to treat as the next archive level. Repeat in stack order.

parameters.name: member
parameters.description: Optional text member path to read as a bounded chunk

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Byte offset inside the selected member; defaults to 0

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum bytes to return, from 1 through 1048576; defaults to 262144

200.description: The archive listing, or a text member chunk when `member` is set

400.description: Bad digest, unsafe filename, or archive nesting depth above the limit

404.description: No file with this digest is known, or the member does not exist

413.description: Nested archive size or listed entries exceed limits

415.description: Not a supported archive type, or the selected member is not text

416.description: The requested member offset is beyond the member size

get.description: Legacy path form for reading one root archive member as bounded text chunks. Prefer the query form when member names contain slashes or when inspecting nested archives.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: The display filename, percent-encoded as one path segment. Separators, traversal, and control characters are rejected.

415.description: The member is not previewable text

416.description: The requested offset is beyond the member size

get.description: The projects peryx has observed on this index: everything uploaded, plus every mirrored project a client has asked for. A virtual index unions its layers. Index policy filters denied projects before serialization. JSON or HTML by `Accept`.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: `application/vnd.pypi.simple.v1+json` for PEP 691 JSON; anything else gets PEP 503 HTML

200.description: The project list (PEP 691 shown; PEP 503 HTML without the JSON `Accept`)

get.description: All files of one project, merged across virtual-index layers (first match per filename wins, versions union). File URLs point back at peryx's own `files/` route; `core-metadata` advertises the PEP 658 sibling. Index policy filters denied files and their versions before serialization.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: `application/vnd.pypi.simple.v1+json` for PEP 691 JSON; anything else gets PEP 503 HTML

403.description: Index policy denied the project detail

404.description: No layer of this index has the project

502.description: The upstream failed and nothing is cached

delete.description: Removes every uploaded file of the project. Requires the local layer to be `volatile`.

200.description: Done; the body counts affected files

403.description: The index is not volatile; delete is disabled

405.description: The route's index has no writable local layer

get.description: PyPI's legacy project JSON shape, built from the resolved Simple API detail page. The `releases` and `urls` file entries preserve hashes, yanked markers, upload time, size, and `requires_python`; metadata that Simple pages do not expose is null, empty, or `-1`.

502.description: The upstream failed and nothing is cached

delete.description: Removes the version's uploaded files outright. Requires the local layer to be `volatile`; for a virtual index, the upstream files become visible again.

200.description: Done; the body counts affected files

403.description: The index is not volatile; delete is disabled

405.description: The route's index has no writable local layer

get.description: Version-specific legacy JSON. This has the same `info`, `urls`, `vulnerabilities`, and `ownership` shape as the project endpoint, without the deprecated `releases` map.

404.description: No layer has the project or version

502.description: The upstream failed and nothing is cached

put.description: Copies uploaded file records for one release from the source route's local upload layer into this route's local upload layer. Promotion reuses content-addressed blob digests and preserves sha256, size, upload time, yank state, and metadata sibling hashes. The target route's upload token authenticates the request. Archived and quarantined target projects reject promotion through the same project-status policy used for uploads.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Source route whose local upload layer contains the release

200.description: Done; the body counts promoted files

400.description: Missing `from`, unsafe path segment, or missing version

403.description: Project status rejects writes on the target route

404.description: Unknown route or no source release matched

405.description: The source or target route has no writable local layer

409.description: A target filename already exists with a different sha256

put.description: Clears the hidden marker a DELETE leaves on files served from a read-only cache, making them visible on the virtual index again. Omit `{version}/` to restore the whole project.

200.description: Done; the body counts affected files

405.description: The route's index has no writable local layer

put.description: Marks the version's files yanked (PEP 592): resolvers skip them, exact-pin installs still succeed. Uploaded files get their record updated; files served from a read-only cache get a reversible override on the virtual index's hosted layer, so upstream releases can be yanked too. Omit `{version}/` (i.e. `PUT /{route}/{project}/yank`) to yank the whole project. Add `?reason=...` to preserve a resolver-visible reason.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Optional PEP 592 yank reason preserved in Simple API output

200.description: Done; the body counts affected files

405.description: The route's index has no writable local layer

delete.description: Clears the PEP 592 yank marker set by the PUT form of this path.

200.description: Done; the body counts affected files

405.description: The route's index has no writable local layer

uploadToken.description: Any username; the password is the hosted index's `upload_token` (the pypi.org `__token__` convention)
